message:
perf: Optimize search command label filtering with batched query

Previously, when filtering by labels (--label or --label-any flags),
the search command would call storage.get_labels() for each issue
individually, resulting in N+1 database queries where N is the number
of issues matching other filters.

This commit introduces a batched approach:
1. Before the filter loop, collect all issue IDs that need label checking
2. Call storage.get_labels_for_issues() once to fetch all labels in
   a single query with batched IN clauses
3. Use a HashMap lookup during filtering instead of per-issue queries

Performance impact:
- Searching 100 issues with label filters: ~100 queries -> 1-2 queries
- Eliminates SQLite round-trip overhead for each issue
- Particularly noticeable on larger repositories with many issues

The batched query already existed (get_labels_for_issues) and was used
by the list command optimization; this brings search in line with that
pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
